change: you change a GovValidator storage slot constant

impact.why: Reads SLOT_VALIDATOR_validators via NewAddressSet; must use the same slot the writer wrote. Read site.
Reads CalculateMappingSlot(SLOT_VALIDATOR_validatorToBlsKey, val); slot-constant read site paired with the init writer.
Reads SLOT_VALIDATOR_gasTip directly; slot-constant read site.
Validator set derivation reads the validators slot via ValidatorList; wrong slot => empty/garbage validator set => consensus failure.
Seal verification fetches each validator's BLS key from the validatorToBlsKey slot; slot change breaks seal checks.
Reads gasTip slot when building WBFTExtra/header; slot change yields wrong tip.
Miner reads SLOT_VALIDATOR_gasTip via GetGasTip when assembling blocks; depends on the slot constant.
Invokes initializeValidator (the writer) during genesis/v1 init; the write side that must stay consistent with all readers and the Solidity layout.

grep_would_miss: engine.go GetValidators/BLS/gasTip sites and miner/worker.go — they call the reader helpers (ValidatorList/GetBLSPublicKey/GetGasTip), never reference SLOT_VALIDATOR_* textually. A grep for the slot constant finds zero of them.
The cross-cutting consensus/miner impact: changing one constant silently desyncs validator-set and seal verification two call hops away from the constant.
The Solidity contract storage layout (off-repo / not a Go symbol) must change in lockstep — noted in the file's own CAUTION comment.
notes: All SLOT_VALIDATOR_* textual references live in gov_validator.go: initializeValidator (writer, the seed — excluded) plus the three reader helpers. Real impact propagates through those readers into consensus engine and miner. blsPop/validatorToOperator/operatorToValidator/blsKeyToValidator slots are written by initializeValidator but have no Go read site in this repo (consumed by the Solidity contract); included implicitly via the writer. cks edges empty; built from ripgrep SLOT_VALIDATOR + reader-caller grep, confirmed by Read.
